data.title: Adobe gains 3% as $25B buyback offsets AI concerns, stock slump
data.description:
Shares of Adobe Inc. rose about 3% on Wednesday after the company unveiled a $25 billion share buyback program, offering a boost to investor sentiment even as concerns around artificial intelligence and competitive pressures continue to weigh on the stock.

The rally comes after a prolonged slump, with Adobe shares down over 20% both year-to-date and over the past 12 months, significantly underperforming the broader S&P 500.

Buyback aims to support sentiment amid stock slump

Adobe’s board authorized the $25 billion repurchase program late Tuesday during an investor session at its annual conference.

The initiative will run through April 30, 2030, and is aimed at minimizing dilution from stock issuances, reducing share count over time, and returning value to investors.

The move could provide a morale boost to shareholders who have endured steep losses in the stock.

While the buyback signals confidence from management, it also reflects a broader trend across the software sector, where companies are leaning on capital returns to stabilize sentiment during periods of uncertainty.

AI strategy in focus as competition intensifies

Adobe continues to face scrutiny over how effectively it can integrate and monetize artificial intelligence within its core business.

While it remains a leader in creative software, investors have grown cautious about rising competition and the pace of AI disruption.

Oppenheimer reiterated a Perform rating on the stock following recent investor presentations, where Adobe outlined its AI strategy and customer experience offerings.

The firm noted strong adoption and monetization trends in AI products, suggesting potential for faster growth if execution remains strong.

At the same time, concerns persist around pricing pressure and the durability of Adobe’s competitive moat as the industry shifts toward AI-driven solutions.

Oppenheimer also flagged risks to fiscal 2026 guidance, particularly in light of a potential reset under new leadership.

Despite the cautious tone, analyst sentiment has shown some improvement, with 26 analysts revising earnings estimates upward for the upcoming period. Adobe also maintains a strong gross profit margin of 89.4%.

Adobe recently announced a new AI agent-based platform, CX Enterprise, aimed at helping businesses automate marketing and customer experience functions.

Leadership transition adds another layer of uncertainty

Adobe is also navigating a leadership transition after announcing that Chief Executive Officer Shantanu Narayen will step down once a successor is named.

This adds another layer of uncertainty for investors already assessing the company’s long-term positioning.

Meanwhile, peers are adopting similar strategies. Salesforce Inc. recently said it would borrow funds to accelerate a $25 billion share repurchase program, highlighting a broader shift within the sector.

As Adobe balances shareholder returns, innovation, and leadership changes, investors will be watching closely to see whether its AI strategy can translate into sustained growth and help reverse its recent stock underperformance.

data.title: Getty Images Cut To Caa1 As $88 Million Ruling And Deal Scrutiny Weigh
data.description:
Getty Images is coming under renewed pressure after Moody's Ratings downgraded the company deeper into junk territory, pointing to weakening liquidity and rising uncertainty around its strategic path. The rating was cut by two notches to Caa1 from B2, with the outlook revised to negative from stable, suggesting risks could continue to build. The agency's view appears tied not only to current balance sheet conditions but also to lingering questions surrounding a potential merger with Shutterstock, which remains unresolved and under increasing scrutiny.

The downgrade follows a legal setback that could add near-term financial strain. A US appeals court denied Getty Images' request for a rehearing in its dispute with investors Alta Partners and CRCM Institutional Master Fund, leaving in place a ruling requiring the company to pay about $88 million plus interest. Getty Images has said it plans to fund the payment by drawing on its $150 million revolving credit facility, while Moody's estimates the total cash outflow could be around $110 million. This approach may address the immediate obligation, but it could also tighten liquidity at a time when financial flexibility is already under pressure.

At the same time, regulatory risks tied to the proposed Shutterstock transaction continue to develop. The UK's Competition and Markets Authority has indicated the deal could result in a substantial lessening of competition in editorial content, triggering an in-depth investigation. Against this backdrop, Getty Images is managing a capital structure that included about $2 billion of debt at the end of 2025, alongside upcoming maturities such as $295 million of unsecured notes due March 2028 and a revolving credit facility linked to refinancing conditions. Taken together, these factors suggest execution over the coming periods could be important in determining whether liquidity pressures stabilize or potentially intensify.

data.title: Goldman Invests $50 Million In BLP Digital As AI Finance Demand Grows
data.description:
A unit of Goldman Sachs Asset Management has taken a $50 million stake in BLP Digital, signaling a growing institutional push into AI-driven finance operations. The investment, executed through Goldman Sachs Alternatives, involves purchasing shares from existing investors, with no valuation disclosed. The move comes as both enterprises and capital providers appear to be increasingly focused on applying AI to core financial workflows, where automation could improve efficiency and strengthen control over cash flow and working capital.

BLP Digital, founded in 2019 by brothers Tim and Sven Beck and spun out of ETH Zurich, has largely scaled without relying on external funding. Its platform uses AI agents to handle tasks such as invoice processing and financial closing, replacing older systems like optical character recognition and robotic process automation. Co-founder Tim Beck indicated the company is growing at over 100% annually, noting that over the past six months the market's understanding of this technology has shifted, with enterprises now more actively adopting these solutions.

For Goldman Sachs Alternatives, which has deployed about $13 billion into high-growth technology companies over the past two decades, the investment could reflect a broader view that demand for AI-enabled financial operations may continue to accelerate. Switzerland's AI ecosystem, while not matching the scale of the US, has been building capabilities in areas like sovereign AI, emphasizing locally controlled systems. As part of its expansion strategy, BLP Digital plans to increase its workforce from around 150 employees to roughly 260 by year-end and to about 500 by 2027, alongside ambitions to establish a stronger presence in the US market and capture a meaningful share of demand.

data.title: Netflix Stock Has Lost Steam. This Next Step Could Heat Things Up Again. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Angela Palumbo

Netflix is launching vertical videos to compete for engagement with social media companies, and this J.P. Morgan analyst thinks thats a solid plan.

Netflix announced when reporting first-quarter earnings on April 16 that it will be launching vertical videos at the end of the month. This video feed will show up on mobile devices and is expected to show user's clips of available content on the Netflix platform.

"We view vertical video as a way to engage users on another screen beyond the TV, and capture share of shorter, snackable moments, which could ultimately lead to increased engagement with NFLX's longer-form content," J.P. Morgan's Doug Anmuth wrote in a research note.

Netflix shareholders are paying close attention to user engagement as competition with other streamers heats up. But it's not just fellow streamers, like Disney+ and HBO Max, that Netflix is competing with for viewership.

Social media takes up a large amount of screen time, especially with younger people who gravitate towards short-form video content. Netflix is hoping this new vertical video strategy can compete with players like TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts for viewership.

Netflix has been working towards changing some of its available content to try and keep users engaged. The platform now hosts certain social media personalities, like Ms. Rachel. Netflix also recently started uploading video podcasts to the platform, and has been hosting a plethora of live sporting events. Anmuth thinks Netflix highlighting this type of content in vertical videos would be a smart move for the company.

"Vertical video could drive increased awareness of content, as well as greater engagement, while helping NFLX reach younger cohorts where they spend time — on mobile devices," Anmuth wrote. He rates Netflix as Overweight with a $118 price target.

Netflix stock has been on a roller coaster this year. Shares slumped after the company entered into an agreement to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery. The stock bounced back after Netflix backed away from the deal. Shares are now down 3% in April after investors responded to the company's disappointing second-quarter financial guidance.

Introducing a new type of content to boost engagement is one way to potentially get Netflix shareholders excited again.

Write to Angela Palumbo at angela.palumbo@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Karin Rosenthal will step down as CFO at Mips AB (publ)
data.description:
Mips AB (publ) today announces that Karin Rosenthal, in mutual agreement with the company, has decided to step down from her position as Chief Financial Officer (CFO) at Mips. Karin has served as CFO since 2020 and will remain in her role until 22 October 2026 to ensure a smooth transition and to support the recruitment and onboarding of her successor. The search process for a new CFO will begin immediately.

“Karin has been an important part of Mips’ journey in recent years, and we will miss a highly competent, dedicated and positive colleague. I would like to extend my warm thanks to Karin for her significant contributions and wish her the very best going forward. The recruitment of a new CFO will begin immediately, and I am confident that Karin and her team will ensure a smooth handover,” says Max Strandwitz, CEO of Mips.

“Mips is a fantastic company, and my time here has been an incredible journey. I am grateful for the years spent working alongside so many skilled and dedicated colleagues, and I am proud of what we have achieved together. The company is entering an exciting phase of development, and I feel the timing is now right to hand over to a new CFO. I will take many warm memories with me and will continue to follow Mips’ journey with great interest,” says Karin Rosenthal, leaving CFO.

For more information, please contact:

Max Strandwitz, President and CEO

Max.Strandwitz@Mipsprotection.com

tel +46 709 61 17 54

Karin Rosenthal, CFOKarin.Rosenthal@Mipsprotection.com

tel +46 768 34 63 66

About Mips Group

Mips Group is a global safety company, based in science, operating with an ingredient brand business model that includes the brands Koroyd and Mips. The Group develops innovative safety solutions that are integrated into helmets and other protective products by renowned brands worldwide. Koroyd is a pioneer in advanced impact protection technology based on aerospace safety research, while Mips is a leader in helmet-based safety backed by over 30 years of research and development in collaboration with the Swedish Royal Institute of Technology and Karolinska Institutet. Koroyd SARL is seated in Monaco, Mips AB is headquartered in Stockholm and listed on Nasdaq Stockholm. For more information, visit www.koroyd.com and www.mipscorp.com.

Attachments

Karin Rosenthal will step down as CFO at Mips AB (publ)

data.title: Canadian dollar steadies as Mideast uncertainty keeps risk appetite in check
data.description:
Key points:
  • Loonie holds near its strongest level since March 13
  • Price of oil rises 4.2%
  • New housing prices fall 0.2% in March
  • Bond yields trade mixed across the curve

By Fergal Smith

The Canadian dollar steadied near a six-week high against its U.S. counterpart on Wednesday as oil prices rose and investors awaited signs of diplomatic progress to end the war in the Middle East.

The loonie was trading nearly unchanged at 1.3660 per U.S. dollar, or 73.21 U.S. cents. On Tuesday, the currency touched its strongest intraday level since March 13 at 1.3629.

"Tuesday’s price action suggests markets remain more focused on global risk appetite than on domestic fundamentals," analysts at Monex Europe said in a note.

Iran seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, tightening its grip on the strategic waterway after U.S. President Donald Trump called off attacks with no sign of peace talks restarting.

"Should a ceasefire extension hold, and oil prices stabilise, we could see CAD recover toward its recent highs. But until there is tangible progress on the diplomatic front, we expect choppy trading conditions to persist," the Monex analysts said.

The safe-haven U.S. dollar rose against a basket of major currencies and the price of oil was trading 4.2% higher at $93.42 a barrel.

Oil is one of Canada's major exports, which have been disrupted in the past year by hefty U.S. tariffs on critical sectors, such as autos, steel and aluminum. The United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement, a continental trade pact, is set for review by a July 1 deadline.

Canada is not a supplicant that will allow the United States to dictate the terms for a review of the USMCA, Prime Minister Mark Carney said.

Domestic data showed that new housing prices fell 0.2% in March from February.

February retail sales data, due on Friday, could offer additional clues on the state of the domestic economy, with economists expecting a monthly increase of 0.9%.

Canadian government bond yields were mixed across the curve, with the 10-year (CA10YT=RR) down less than a basis point at 3.478%.

data.title: Aberdeen Reports 2.9 Billion Outflows As Assets Rebound To 573 Billion
data.description:
Aberdeen Group Plc (SLFPF) is continuing to face client withdrawals as volatility linked to the US-Iran war weighed on flows in the first quarter. The firm reported net outflows of 2.9 billion, compared with 2.5 billion in the prior quarter, with pressure concentrated in its asset management division. Management indicated that 4 billion of previously announced redemptions in lower-margin equities, alongside adverse market movements, drove much of the decline, although this was partly offset by net inflows into fixed income strategies.

Assets under management and administration declined 1.5% through the end of March, though the firm noted a subsequent recovery of 4.6% to 573 billion as of April 17, suggesting some improvement as market conditions stabilized. At the same time, Interactive Investor continued to gain traction, with customer numbers rising 14% year over year and delivering a record quarter for both net inflows and average daily retail trades. This dynamic points to a growing divergence between institutional outflows and retail-led growth within the group.

Chief Executive Officer Jason Windsor is increasingly positioning Aberdeen toward wealth management, with Interactive Investor providing a more consistent source of inflows while efforts continue to reshape the adviser unit and the asset management business after years of withdrawals. The transition comes alongside leadership changes, with Jonathan Asquith appointed as temporary chair as the firm searches for a permanent successor to Douglas Flint, who is set to join Prudential Plc. Aberdeen's share price has risen about 70% from its April 2025 low and is up 2.5% since the start of the year, a move that could reflect improving sentiment as investors assess the progress of the turnaround strategy.

data.title: EU wheat eases as supply prospects offset Iran war worries
data.description:
Euronext wheat edged lower on Wednesday as forecasts of rain in parched U.S. wheat belts and an increased estimate of Russia's next harvest reinforced expectations of ample global supply and took attention away from war tensions in the Middle East.

September futures (BL2U6), now the most-active position on Euronext, settled 0.2% down at 208.50 euros ($244.15), after reaching a two-week high of 210.25 euros.

Latest weather charts showed significant rainfall may reach dry parts of the U.S. Plains in the coming two weeks, potentially easing what has been the main crop concern in the northern hemisphere.

Crude oil rose as reports of gunfire attacks on several ships in the Strait of Hormuz tempered reaction to Washington's announcement that it would indefinitely extend a ceasefire with Iran.

Concerns that the Iran war will inflate harvest production costs have supported grain prices during the conflict.

In Germany, traders said cost inflation was making farmers reluctant to release supplies as they hold out for better prices.

“An indirect impact of the Iran war is unwillingness of German farmers to sell old-crop wheat, making it difficult to gather supplies to meet export demand visible from west Africa for June shipment,” one German trader said.

In limited export demand, an Algerian buyer was seeking about 30,000 tons of Black Sea wheat at about $265 a ton cost and freight (c&f) included for June shipment. A Turkish buyer was seeking about 10,000 tons of Black Sea 11.5% protein wheat at around $245 a ton c&f for May shipment.

“Turkey’s wheat crop appears to be developing very well and market expectations are growing that Turkey will restrict wheat imports from June or July if a large crop actually arrives,” another trader said.

A rebound in grain production in Morocco, which the government expects to double this year after a long-term drought ended, could also curb demand from what has been the main export outlet for European Union wheat.

Weekly EU data showed that the bloc's soft wheat exports so far this season had reached 19 million tons, up 8% from a year ago, though figures for several countries remained incomplete.

Financial investors switched to a net short position in Euronext wheat last week, data from the exchange showed.

($1 = 0.8540 euros)

data.title: UK pension investor Nest to oppose BP Chair's re-election
data.description:
By Simon Jessop

UK pension investor Nest plans to oppose the re-election of BP Chair Albert Manifold at the company's annual meeting on Thursday, it said on Wednesday, citing board actions that it said risked limiting shareholder engagement.

Those include the exclusion from the meeting of a shareholder resolution proposed by activist Follow This, asking for more disclosure on how BP's strategy would stand up under scenarios of declining demand for oil and gas.

Other steps include a move to retire two previous resolutions requiring company-specific climate reporting, backed by more than 97% of shareholders, and a proposal for virtual AGMs, which could reduce transparency and oversight, Nest said.

"Investors look to the Chair to safeguard governance and shareholder rights. In this instance, that expectation has not been met," said Diandra Soobiah, director of responsible investment at Nest.

Nest held about 40.5 million pounds ($54.7 million) in BP stock at the end of March.

"Following extensive engagement with our largest investors we are fully focused on building a simpler, stronger and more valuable BP. That's why we are making these recommendations, to provide transparent, standardised disclosures that support clear comparisons across companies," BP said in a statement.

($1 = 0.7406 pounds)

data.title: Honeywell's Quantinuum is making a bold statement with its upcoming IPO
data.description:
By Hannah Pedone

Quantinuum is going the traditional IPO route, while other quantum companies have gone public through SPACs. The move bolsters the company's credibility.

Honeywell announced on Wednesday that Quantinuum has filed for an IPO.

In what could be a big credibility boost for the industry, Honeywell International's quantum-computing business is about to go public.

Honeywell (HON) announced on Wednesday that its subsidiary Quantinuum has submitted confidential paperwork for an initial public offering.

Honeywell had previously announced plans to submit the registration in January. Details of the number of shares and price range for the offering were not specified.

The move "legitimizes" quantum companies and encourages public markets to view the technology as a "proven sector rather than a speculative one," Wedbush analyst Antoine Legault told MarketWatch.

"Nearly every single public quantum company" has debuted via a merger with a special-purpose acquisition corporation, Legault said. Those companies were able to bypass some of the scrutiny that comes with the IPO process, so it's notable that Quantinuum is breaking from the pack and pursuing a traditional offering.

The choice shows that the company wants to position itself as a "mature, well-funded, higher-quality asset" with strong backing from Honeywell, according to Legault. It also sends a message that the company doesn't need to fast-track its public debut.

Quantinuum, which was formed in 2021 by the merger of Honeywell Quantum Solutions and Cambridge Quantum, was valued at $10 billion in a fundraising round in September.

The full-stack quantum company markets itself as the world's "largest standalone integrated quantum computing company." Its CEO, Rajeeb Hazra, previously held leadership roles at Micron Technology (MU) and Intel (INTC).

Last year, the company announced the launch of its commercial quantum computer, Helios. Quantinuum has a partnership to integrate the computer with Nvidia's (NVDA) artificial-intelligence supercomputing technology.

Honeywell CEO Vimal Kapur said on the company's last earnings call that Quantinuum's design and software stack aims to bring quantum programming "closer to the ease and flexibility of classical computing" and to lead to commercial adoption of the technology. He added that Quantinuum's commercial applications have drawn interest from banks, pharmaceutical companies and public-sector agencies.

Honeywell plans to report earnings on Thursday morning.

-Hannah Pedone

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
